raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
January 2026 closed with actual spend and revenue of **£8,546** against a full-period budget of **£580**, producing a favourable net variance of **+£7,966 (+1,373.4%)**. The entirety of the significant variance is attributable to a single line: Sales outperformed budget by **+£5,121 (+1,024.1%)**, driven by the recognition of five deals — three of which were Q4 2025 carry-overs that slipped past the 31 December close date — alongside two further inbound wins secured in mid-January. The January Sales budget of £500 was set as a conservative placeholder for a traditionally quiet month and did not anticipate this pipeline acceleration. The variance is expected and has been formally approved by the CFO. The remaining variance across all other lines is immaterial and carries no significant risk items requiring executive attention.

LINE COMMENTARY

Sales | +£5,121 (+1,024.1%) | Budget: £500 | Actual: £5,621
Sales exceeded budget by £5,121, representing a 1,024.1% favourable variance. The driver is the recognition of five deals totalling £5,621 in January, against a budget of £500 set as a conservative placeholder for a seasonally quiet month.
Three deals are confirmed Q4 2025 pipeline carry-overs that did not close before 31 December 2025: Redwood Group (£2,100 ARR, closed 8 Jan), Apex Analytics (£1,850 ARR, closed 8 Jan), and Fortis Ltd (£1,300 ARR, closed 9 Jan). Two additional inbound deals — Crest Digital (£220) and BlueSky Ops (£151) — closed 12–15 January, bringing the total recognised revenue to £5,621, which reconciles exactly to the reported actual.
A CEO pipeline carry-over memo and CFO revenue recognition sign-off both confirm the variance was anticipated and formally approved. As these deals represent catch-up from a prior period rather than sustained demand acceleration, the forward budget may warrant review to assess whether the January run-rate is representative of the ongoing pipeline.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-05T26:15:00Z - crm-sales-broad - crm-q4-carryover: CRM pipeline report January 2026. Redwood Group — Closed Won 8 Jan (ARR £2,100). Apex Analytics — Closed Won 8 Jan (ARR £1,850). Fortis Ltd — Closed Won 9 Jan (ARR £1,300). Total carry-over from Q4 2025: £5,250.
- Gmail - 2024-11-05T18:15:00Z - gmail-sales-broad - gmail-2026-01-08-pipeline: Q4 pipeline carry-over memo from CEO — three major deals (Redwood, Apex, Fortis) that were forecast to close by 31 December 2025 have now signed in January. Revenue recognition approved by CFO. January budget variance is expected and approved.
- Slack - 2024-11-05T12:15:00Z - slack-sales-broad - sales-slack-2026-01-08: Big news — three Q4 deals that didn't close before Christmas have landed in January. Redwood, Apex, and Fortis all signed this week. Combined first-month billing £3,200. The January budget was set at £500 assuming a quiet month — looks like the pipeline catch-up exceeded expectations.
- CRM - 2024-11-05T26:45:00Z - crm-sales-narrow - crm-jan-additional: Two additional inbound deals closed Jan 12–15: Crest Digital £220, BlueSky Ops £151. Total January pipeline recognition £5,621.
- Gmail - 2024-11-05T18:45:00Z - gmail-sales-narrow - gmail-2026-01-15-rev-sign-off: CFO sign-off on January revenue: £5,621 recognised. January plan was £500 (conservative placeholder for traditionally slow month). Variance is fully explained by Q4 carry-over.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines produced immaterial variances in January 2026 and require no further commentary at this time:
- **Audit & Accountancy Fees** — £47 adverse
- **Bank Fees** — £15 adverse
- **Entertainment – 100% Business** — £13 adverse
- **General Expenses** — £308 adverse
- **Light Power Heating** — £49 adverse (+61.7% against a small budget base)
- **Motor Vehicle Expenses** — £124 adverse
- **Printing & Stationery** — £18 adverse
- **Rent** — £984 adverse
- **Subscriptions** — £1,220 adverse
- **Telephone & Internet** — £37 adverse
- **Travel National** — £29 adverse
